Method–provenance block:
Property: atom-resolved Fukui functions (f⁺, f⁻, dual descriptor) and most-reactive sites for phenol.
Level of theory: DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP), exact four-center integrals (density fitting OFF) in PySCF 2.x as wrapped by chemkit.
Geometry provenance: fixed user-supplied coordinates (phenol.xyz at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/phenol/phenol.xyz); single-point neutral (N), cationic (N–1), and anionic (N+1) calculations performed at the same geometry (vertical ionizations/attachments). No geometry optimization was carried out (Fukui = DFT//input_geometry).
Solvent / environment: gas phase (implicit solvation not invoked).
Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet parent (charge 0, multiplicity 1); cation charge +1, doublet; anion charge –1, doublet.
Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill → PySCF backend.
Convergence & warnings: all three SCF calculations converged; integrity.status = "ok", trustworthy = true.  Warnings returned by the engine are reproduced verbatim below.

Headline result
The most electrophilic site is carbon 3 (index 2) with f⁺ = 2.1028319 (electron affinity descriptor).  The most nucleophilic site is the phenolic oxygen (index 0) with f⁻ = 0.16201477 e.
